category_title: Students with ASD preoccupied with neatness and accuracy
scenario_title: Math game on Nintendo DS with perfectionism and error-triggered restarts
minimal_context: The student is completing the x20 math game on a Nintendo DS using a stylus while a timer runs on the desk.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Please start the x20 math game on your Nintendo DS. I am setting the timer for the session.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Watches the timer on the desk, then slowly picks up the stylus to write the first answer very carefully.)

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Takes a long time writing the number, trying to make it perfectly neat on the touch screen.)

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Looks distracted, writes an incorrect answer, and immediately restarts the entire x20 game.)

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: Please start the x20 math game on your Nintendo DS. I am setting the timer for the session.

STUDENT: (Watches the timer on the desk, then slowly picks up the stylus to write the first answer very carefully.)

TUTOR: Try the next math problem.

STUDENT: (Takes a long time writing the number, trying to make it perfectly neat on the touch screen.)

TUTOR: Keep going.

STUDENT: (Looks distracted, writes an incorrect answer, and immediately restarts the entire x20 game.)

task.content: Answering math sums using a stylus on the DS touch screen in the x20 game, and completing the Westwood One Minute Test of Basic Number Facts
task.learning_goal: Recognising numbers, recognising maths symbols, matching maths symbols, and solving single digit addition, subtraction and multiplication problems

context.materials: Nintendo DS console with Dr Kawashima’s Brain Training game, stylus, calculator, timer, paper and pencil

challenging_behavior_trigger.condition: Encountering an error (incorrect answer) while playing the x20 game on the Nintendo DS
challenging_behavior_trigger.observable_behavior: Would tend to restart the x20 if she answered an item incorrectly

behavior_patterns.condition: Writing answers to math problems on the Nintendo DS touch screen using a stylus
behavior_patterns.internal_state: Very preoccupied with writing neatly and correctly

behavior_patterns.condition: Doing the standardised test while being timed
behavior_patterns.observable_behavior: Played with the timer or watched it during the test
behavior_patterns.internal_state: Very anxious and distracted by the timer
